title: MatchRecord
description: A portable, publishable record of one SRG Supershow match — the interchange format for replay (src/record.rs, DESIGN.md §8.1). A record is an ordered sequence of observable FRAMES (public state + the action that produced it) plus participants and a result. Engine-run ('full') records also carry a compact replay seed that re-simulates the match exactly; transcribed ('observer') records — a real-life or other-platform match a human archived — carry frames only and are playback-only. NO record of either kind contains a hidden zone: hands and decks appear as counts, and a card movement that was private end-to-end appears as a count with no card ids. schema_version mirrors src/record.rs RECORD_SCHEMA_VERSION.

kind.description: 'full' = produced by this engine, re-simulatable from `replay`. 'observer' = transcribed from a match the engine did not run; no seed, no hidden state, playback only.

engine.description: The producing engine's version stamp (srg_core::version_info() — the same object `srg info` and the WASM `version()` emit). Present on engine-produced records; replay fidelity is only guaranteed against a matching stamp. Absent on observer records.

frames.description: The playback layer: chronological, dense, 0-based `seq`. The first frame is the opening position ('start'); the last is the 'result' action and must agree with the record's `result`.

replay.description: The compact re-simulation seed (session snapshot: seed + both enriched decks + seats + the recorded remote decisions). 'full' records only — an observer record MUST NOT carry one, since an observed match cannot be re-derived. Feed it to Session::restore / WasmSession.restore.

Meta.description: Descriptive only; nothing here feeds the engine.

source.description: Provenance — 'get-diced.com Run It Back', 'in person — locals 2026-07-19'.

Participant.description: One seat's identity. `deck` is optional — most transcribed matches do not know the opponent's full decklist.

player.description: The human or AI behind the seat ('Brandon', 'heuristic').

CardRef.description: A card's database identity plus optional display hints. `card` is the db_uuid — the join key against the card DB (get-diced.com); an importer that could not identify a card may leave it empty, which the validator reports as a warning. The engine always fills `name`/`number`.

card.description: db_uuid, or "" for an unidentified card.

Frame.description: One replay step: the public state at the moment of an action. State is captured AS OF the action, not after everything it triggers has settled — a played card is still resolving through the stop window and reaches `in_play` in a later frame.

PlayerFrame.description: One seat's public position. The closed zones appear only as sizes — there is no field that can carry hand or deck CONTENTS, which is what makes a record safe to publish. The size fields are optional because a spectator transcribing a live match may not have them; the engine always writes them.

in_play.description: The open board, in play order.

Action.description: What happened, tag-dispatched by `type` under the same names the game log uses (schemas/v1/gamelog.schema.json). Log events an observer could not see have no action: `decision` (its legal list enumerates the deciding player's hand) and `unsupported` (an engine diagnostic) are dropped, not redacted.

oneOf.title: start
oneOf.description: The opening position, before anything happened.

skill.description: Power | Agility | Technique | Submission | Grapple | Strike.

player.description: The seat playing the stop card.

tie_bumps.description: How many bumps the roll-off needed.

count.description: Deck→hand is private on both ends: a count, never card ids.

oneOf.title: effect

src.description: The seat whose card fired the effect.

target.description: The seat the effect landed on.

oneOf.$comment: The engine's `detail` payload is deliberately NOT carried: it is bookkeeping and can name cards in hidden zones. The frame's own zones show the consequence.

oneOf.title: note
oneOf.description: Free-text colour for something this vocabulary does not model. Never emitted by the engine — it exists so an importer never has to distort a real match.

oneOf.title: result
oneOf.description: The final frame's action; must agree with the record's `result`.

Movement.title: discard | bury | search
Movement.description: A card movement between zones. `cards` is present when the move was publicly visible and omitted (a bare `count`) when the game log marked it hidden — both endpoints private. `from` names the origin zone or deck end (e.g. 'hand', 'in_play', 'discard', 'deck', 'TOP', 'BOTTOM').

reason.description: How it ended. The engine produces finish | count_out | disqualification | pinfall | turn_cap; an imported record may use another string, which the validator reports as a warning.
